title: Taeniopsis revoluta
taxonomy: Taeniopsis revoluta is classified as a species within the family Cunoniaceae, a family of flowering plants native to the Southern Hemisphere. The Cunoniaceae family is part of the order Oxalidales, which also includes other plant families such as Oxalidaceae and Elaeocarpaceae. This classification suggests that Taeniopsis revoluta shares characteristics with other plants in the Oxalidales order. The classification of Taeniopsis revoluta is further refined to the genus level, which groups it with other similar species. At the species level, Taeniopsis revoluta is a distinct entity within its genus.

intro: Taeniopsis revoluta, also known as the Japanese tallow tree or kusunoki, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Taeniopsis revoluta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Taeniopsis revoluta is often prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to make infusions and tinctures for the treatment of various health conditions.
